task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the required climate datasets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and the datasets 'air.mon.ltm.nc' and 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' are visible in the Sources/Datasets tree on the left.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D line plot for the 12-month temperature profile at 5°N, 75°W.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 1D line graph (typically with months on the X-axis and temperature on the Y-axis). The plot settings or title must indicate it is for 'air' temperature at Latitude 5°N and Longitude 75°W (or 285°E).

task_completion.description: Create a 1D line plot for the 12-month precipitation profile at 5°N, 75°W.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a 1D line graph for precipitation rate ('prate'). The plot settings or title must indicate it is at Latitude 5°N and Longitude 75°W (or 285°E).

task_completion.description: Save the generated 1D plots as PNG files in the AgriRisk directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the files 'colombia_temp_annual.png' and 'colombia_precip_annual.png' successfully saved in the '~/Documents/AgriRisk/' directory.

task_completion.description: Complete and save the veto memo explaining topographic smoothing artifacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'veto_memo.txt' saved in '~/Documents/AgriRisk/'. The text must contain an explanation of how coarse grid resolution or topographic smoothing affects local temperature readings.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Panoply Java interface being interacted with (menus, plot windows, array settings) rather than a Python script or terminal commands generating the plots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively edited the memo to include the methodological explanation.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) should be visible with the agent typing or having typed the explanation about grid-cell averaging and resolution, rather than just copying a blank template.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted data for the exact requested coordinates.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the Panoply plot windows, the 'Array 1' tab or the plot subtitles must explicitly show the coordinates set to 5 N and 75 W (which may be represented as 285 E in the NetCDF file).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output directory: ~/Documents/AgriRisk/. Expected files: colombia_temp_annual.png, colombia_precip_annual.png, veto_memo.txt. The memo should contain keywords related to topographic smoothing (e.g., resolution, coarse, smooth, average, elevation, grid, topography, scale, mountain, andes). CRITICAL NOTE: The task description implies the dataset shows an artifactually high temperature (>23°C) for 5N, 75W (285E). However, the actual dataset value is exactly 17.58°C. The agent may report 17.58°C (correct data extraction) or >23°C (hallucinating to match the prompt's premise). Both are acceptable for visual verification of the memo's completion, provided the methodological explanation of coarse grid resolution artifacts is present.
